questions.q: What is the difference between a Custom Audience and a Lookalike Audience?
options: Custom is new prospects, Lookalike is existing customers
Custom is people who have ALREADY interacted with the business; Lookalike is NEW people similar to the source
They are the same thing with a different name
questions.explanation: Custom = retargeting. Lookalike = prospecting for new, similar people.

questions.q: Absolute minimum number of people in the source to create a Lookalike:
questions.explanation: 100 people from a SINGLE country. Recommended 1,000-50,000 for quality.

questions.q: Which of these are audience CONTROLS (rigid, cannot be expanded)? Select 2.
questions.explanation: Location and exclusions are rigid controls. Detailed targeting, Lookalike inclusion and age suggestion are suggestions (flexible, AI can expand).

options: Is more similar to the source
Has greater reach but less similarity to the source
questions.explanation: 10% = more reach, less similar. 1% = maximum similarity, smaller reach.

questions.q: The source audience used to create a Lookalike, is it included or excluded?
questions.explanation: The source is ALWAYS excluded. The Lookalike looks for new, similar people; it does not re-reach the source.

questions.q: What is the maximum number of Custom Audiences per ad account and of Lookalikes per source?
questions.explanation: 500 Custom Audiences per ad account and up to 500 Lookalikes per source audience.

questions.q: What is Advantage+ Audience and how is it turned off (intentionally or not)?
options: It can only be turned off in Reservation
It is the AI that expands on your suggestions. It is turned off by choosing 'Switch to original audience options' or 'Further limit the reach of your ads'
It is always ON with no option to turn it off
questions.explanation: On by default. It is turned off intentionally (with those clicks) or accidentally when you manually limit the audience.

questions.q: For Advantage+ Catalog Ads, what is the recommended minimum number of products per Product Set?
questions.explanation: 20 products minimum recommended for enough variety for the ML.

questions.q: Ideal catalog match rate and what problem it indicates if it is low:
options: >75%, a Pixel setup problem (incorrect IDs, missing products)
questions.explanation: >75% target. Low = a mapping problem between Pixel/CAPI and the catalog (Content IDs do not match).

questions.q: An advertiser with several product lines. Best practice?
options: Create one catalog per line for separation
Create a single catalog with Product Sets per line
questions.explanation: A SINGLE catalog with internal Product Sets. Multiple catalogs confuse the matching with the Pixel and complicate the Advantage+ Catalog Ads setup.

questions.q: A business uploads items to its catalog via a data feed and does not know how to write the price. What price format does the product feed support?
questions.explanation: The valid format is the number with a decimal point followed by the ISO currency code: '5.99 USD'. Writing out the currency name ('US Dollars'), using the symbol ('$5.99') or a decimal comma ('5,99') is not the format accepted by the catalog data feed.
